properties.remark: Mudslide with dirt and rock debris reported on road.
Large tree and rocks on SR 38. Angeles Oaks reports 2.35 inch rainfall past 36 hours.
Mud slide covering lanes. Lytle Creek Rd and Glen Helen Pkwy. 3.3 inches at Glen Helen and 4.7 inches in Lytle creek past 48 hours, most of it today.
SR 243 and Vista Grande has rocks on lanes. 2 inches of rain past 24 hours.
Report of mudslide, with dirt and rock debris flow.
Report of mudslide, with dirt and rock debris flow onto Lytle Creek road.
Report of about 5 or 6 boulders in lanes due to debris flow.
Report of about 5 or 6 boulders in lanes due to debris flow. 5 to 6 inches of rain past 2 days.

State route 330 is closed on February 14. Just after 7 pm February 13, multiple vehicles were floating in a ravine full of water, mud and debris. A couple vehicles had occupants in them. Videos available. This is the Line burn scar. This area drains into City creek.
SR 18 and School Road. 6.3 inches of rain past 2 days.
Reported rock run.
Reported rock run at SR 38 and Forest Falls road. 6.8 inches of rain past 2 days.
3 vehicles reported to have been stuck due to debris flow from flash flood.
Report of rock and debris flow in lanes.
Report of rock and debris flow in lanes on Sunset Avenue and Mesa Street. 3 to 4 inches of rain past 2 days.
Mudslide with dirt and rock debris flow. Woods Valley road and Country Oaks Lane. 2 inches of rain from main atmospheric river.
Report of a mudslide with dirt and rock debris flow.
Report of a lot of boulders and debris over the roadway.
Report of a lot of boulders and debris over the roadway. South grade road and Canfield road. 5 to 6 inches of rain past 2 days.
State route 330 remains closed due to Thursday evenings flash flooding.

Corrects time for previous flash flood report from 6 NNE Mission Beach. 4 vehicles submerged in water up to the windows. Lifeguards rescued several. Rainfall rate as high as 1.1 inch in 1 hour. La Jolla Village and Villa La Jolla drives.

Carroll Canyon Road and Pacific Heights Boulevard. 2 vehicles submerged in water rescued by lifeguard boat. GW3482 weather station recorded 1.22 inches between 8 and 9 pm. Other stations recorded 1.07 and 1.16 inches. Miramar MCAS recorded 0.99 inches in the same period.
Tree branches down and patio furniture flipped over.
Top 10 feet of 40 foot palm tree snapped. Report from public.
4 vehicles submerged in water up to the windows. Lifeguards rescued several. Rainfall rate as high as 1.1 inch in 1 hour. La Jolla Village and Villa La Jolla drives.

Delayed report. Swift water rescues. Vehicles in mud, debris and water. 6 persons affected. Mud was half way up the side of one vehicle and drivers were trapped. 3 automated weather stations recorded 0.5 inches in 1 hour. Several mud slides on Soboba Road and Chabela Drive. Non burn scar area. NBC LA and County of Riverside EMD had videos.
Orange County Public Works reported rocks, large tree sections & several inches of mud on Trabuco Canyon Rd.
Time of occurrence is approximate. Dust devil did damage to a carport.
SDGE Descanso station reported a thunderstorm wind gust of 58 mph.
SDHGA Horse Mesonet reported a thunderstorm wind gust of 62 mph near Pine Valley.
SCE Powerline Rd station reported a thunderstorm wind gust of 58 mph.
SCE Ray St mesonet near Perris reported a thunderstorm wind gust of 76 mph.
Rocks and debris reported along Sage Rd at Highway 79 and along Sage Rd between Lewis Rd and Cottonwood School.
Report of a tree down blocking the Emerett Ln in Perris. Nearby SCE Orange Avenue station reported a peak wind gust of 41 mph at 420 PM.
Sand across Highway 18 between Cushenbury and Lucerne Valley due to heavy rain.
Mesonet station 645SE Banning (SCE1645). Reported thunderstorm wind gust.
Thunderstorm outflow wind gusts occurred across this region with gusts 50-60 MPH. Highland Springs station reported a gust to 72 MPH.
Large tree reported down across lanes at the intersection of Gamble Ave and Van Buren Blvd.
Anza Borrego Desert State Part Observation Station reported wind gust of 64 mph.
San Bernardino County reported mudslides blocking SR-38 from Valley of the Falls to the bottom of South Fork.
Report of mudslide and debris flow with powerlines compromised. This has caused Oak Glenn Rd to be closed from Chagall to Potato Canyon.
Mudslide reported near the Oak Glen Steakhouse.
SCE Painted Canyon Rd observation station reported wind gust of 62 mph.
Large boulder in upbound lane of Highway 18 west of Big Bear Lake.
WASH BETWEEN QUAIL HAVEN AND DESERT FRONT COMPLETLY FLOODED DEBRIS ACROSS ROAD.
Part of Worsley Rd/20th Ave is washed out with large rocks and debris in the roadway.
Mud, dirt, and rocks over the roadway.
Report of boulders in the roadway on Highway 138 between Silverwood Lake and Crestline.
Report photos show umbrellas blown from second floor of home to ground, including a bent flag pole hinge from wind.
Based on public captured footage, approximate location and time (south of Coronado, between 12-1:15 PM). No apparent radar signature showing rotation, only a weak shower around 12:05pm in the area.
Multiple photos taken of a watersport west of Point Loma.

ABC7 news reported flooding of Edwards St north of the 405 in Westminster. Video showed approximately 6-12 inches of water on the road. Westminster ALERT gauge reported 1.45 inches of rain in 42 minutes.
Sheep creek is leaving the main channel resulting in flood impacts to Phelan Rd and Hwy 18. Quick maps reports a mud slide across HWY 18 in the High Desert.
Excessive mud and debris running onto North and Southbound I-15 where I-15 intersects HWY 138 through the Cajon Pass.

All lanes of I-15 northbound south of SR-138 are shut down for debris clean up. Traffic is being diverted off at SR-138. Mormon Rock RAWS station (near Cajon Junction) upstream of the road closure had received 7.42 inches of rain between 6-11 AM.

Major mud and debris flow in Wrightwood CA, resulted in damage to multiple properties as well as multiple swiftwater rescues. Road was washed out at sheep creek and Lone Pine rd, along with major impacts to HWY 138. An evacuation warning was issued in Wrightwood from Pine Street to the LA County line. Stations in wrightwood recorded 9.92 inches of rain in 24-hr.

Mt Baldy Road severely impacted and closed in places due to flooding and road washouts. Mt. Baldy rd at Shin Rd was closed due to multiple land and mudslides. Stations in the area recorded 5.75-6.5 inches of rain in 24 hours.
Mt. Baldy Road severely impacted by debris flow.

A new bridge and its construction site along the Glen Helen Parkway at the entrance of the Cajon Pass north of Rialto was completely overwhelmed with floodwaters washing things away and nearly toppling a truck trailer. 4 to 8 inches of rain fell north of the park area, up to 12 inches in Lytle creek, in 24 hours.
Multiple reports of Happy Jack bridge washed out from rushing water in Lytle Creek. Area recorded 8-12 inches of rain in 24 hours.
Corrects rainfall amount in previous flood report from 2 SE Lytle Creek. Reports of multiple homes flooded in the Lytle Creek area (at least 3). Area received 8-12 inches of rain over 24 hours.
